# Circular Dependencies
*or what to Do When It Can’t Untangle the Graph*

This scenario demonstrates its handling of circular dependencies.

#### Dependency Graph
```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 4.4
graph LR
    Root[Project Root] --> LibA[lib-a:1.0.0]
    LibA --> LibB[lib-b:1.0.0]
    LibB --> LibA
```

#### Gradle (Groovy DSL)

```{.groovy include="/demos/gradle-demo/scenario-10-circular/build.gradle" snippet="s10-deps"}
```

#### Expected Intuition
"It should work."

#### Actual Resolution Results
Resolves successfully.

#### Classpath Reality
Both jars present.

#### Why This Happens
It handles cycles gracefully.

#### How Developers Commonly "Fix" This
N/A

#### Safer Ways to Take Control
Avoid cycles.

#### Signals to Watch For
N/A

#### How This Scales in Real Systems
N/A

#### Key Takeaway
It tolerates cycles.

#### Related Scenarios
N/A
